footer.disclaimer: This site is not financial advice. It is a personal, informational tool only — always verify real terms with your bank, lender, or a qualified financial advisor before making any decision.

apartmentTab.title: Apartment
apartmentTab.note: Where you live before buying and what you're selling is now a per-plan decision, not a global setting — set it in the “Plans” tab, separately for each plan.

moneyTab.indexationHint: Free cash flow steps up once a year, in June (the first is June 2027). Separate from the housing market: salary follows salaries, not apartment prices.
moneyTab.rentHint: Paid only while the apartment is sold and a new one is not yet bought. Indexed at the apartment growth rate — it is the price of the same market, and has no rate of its own.
moneyTab.depositHint: One deposit for everything: today's savings, sale proceeds, and monthly contributions. In the Otbasy plan, savings go to the Otbasy account instead. Add your own deposit in the “Deposits” tab.
moneyTab.existingNote: In month 0 everything merges into one deposit — the one chosen above for the Halyk and all-cash plans, the Otbasy account for the Otbasy plan. So only the total matters, not which bank holds it today. Only Otbasy stands apart: it has its own rate and its own CC.
moneyTab.savingsHint: Everything not on the Otbasy account, as one sum.
moneyTab.otbasyAccruedHint: How much the bank has credited so far. CC is computed from exactly this, so starting from zero would push the CC ≥ 5 threshold further out than it really is. Not added to the money — it is already inside the balance.
moneyTab.otbasyMonthsHint: Has no effect yet — recorded for later, against Otbasy's minimum saving term.

depositsTab.builtInNote: From data/deposits.yml. They cannot be changed or removed here — edit the file, and the edit reaches everyone.
depositsTab.ownEmpty: Nothing yet. Add a deposit if you have a bank offer not listed above — and pick it as the main one in the “Money” tab.
depositsTab.removeTitleDisabled: Cannot remove: all the money currently goes to it
depositsTab.payoutHint: 1 = withdraw any time, lose nothing. More — interest is forfeited on early withdrawal.

loansTab.otbasySeedHint: Without seeding, the 50% threshold takes years to reach and rent eats it all.

plansTab.builtInNote: From data/plans.yml. They cannot be changed or removed. Check which ones to show on the chart and in the summary.
plansTab.ownEmpty: Nothing yet. Build your own plan out of four decisions — e.g. “Halyk, but with a 50% down payment” — and compare it against the built-ins.
plansTab.saveMonthsHint: Empty = wait exactly as long as Otbasy does.
plansTab.showMax: Chart shows at most {max} — unpick another plan first

runControls.growthHint: Year over year: 12% = a year from now the price is 12% higher. Steps once every six months. The same rate indexes rent and the price of your apartment being sold. Use a long-run average, not a peak year: the threshold where conclusions flip is ≈9%, and in the 8–10% range the answer is decided by model details, not the market.
runControls.horizonHint: A ceiling only: the calculation still cuts off once the last plan clears its debt. Worth raising if someone doesn't finish paying it off in time.

summaryTable.title: Comparing the options
summaryTable.subtitle: Target loan {loan} ₸ · compared over {months} months (up to the month the last option clears its debt)
summaryTable.dropped: Not counted: {list}. This plan waits exactly as long as Otbasy, and Otbasy never reaches a purchase within the horizon — so there is nothing to wait for. Set a saving window manually or raise the horizon.

scheduleTable.title: Month by month
scheduleTable.note: The purchase row is highlighted. Income is shown in the month interest is paid out, not accrued. Columns that are empty for this option are hidden.

netWorthChart.title: Net worth over time
netWorthChart.subtitle: Apartment + deposits − debt. Continuous across the sale and purchase months. The chart cuts off at month {month} — that's where the last option clears its debt, and there is nothing further to compare.
netWorthChart.empty: No plan is selected. Check plans above the chart or in the “Plans” tab.
netWorthChart.ariaLabel: Net worth by option over time

format.payoutMonthly: paid out every month, withdraw and lose nothing

repayLabels.lump: save up and clear at once
